header_normalization: trim, collapse internal whitespace/newlines to a single space, uppercase
source_file_reference: PEPE BIHAR DSR REPORT 2026.xlsb, sheet 'PEPE DSR- 2026'

financial_year.reason: Pepe's file has no financial_year column, unlike every other brand where FY is supplied and trusted as-is. Confirmed with product owner on 2026-07-05: derive FY from the MONTH column for Pepe only. This is a documented, brand-scoped exception to the general 'never derive season/FY/month' rule -- it does not apply to any other brand.
financial_year.parse: MONTH is formatted '<MONTH NAME>- <YYYY>', e.g. 'JANUARY- 2026' (confirmed in real sample data covering Jan-Apr 2026)
financial_year.rule: financial_year (Apr-Mar) of the parsed month/year; Jan/Feb/Mar YYYY -> FY (YYYY-1)-YYYY, Apr..Dec YYYY -> FY YYYY-(YYYY+1)
financial_year.output_format: YY-YY+1 (2-digit-2-digit, matching Killer's supplied convention, e.g. '25-26')

validation_rules.is_return_when: quantity < 0 OR mrp_value < 0 (real data has both return conventions)
validation_rules.divide_by_zero_guard: if mrp_value == 0, discount_pct is null, not an error
notes: WAD is stored as a fraction (e.g. 0.30007...) not a percentage in the real file; multiply by 100 before comparing to the computed discount_pct.
BillNo is mixed-type in the real file (mostly text like 'PRHO26-79718', occasionally a bare number like 6045); always cast to string, never numeric.
STOCKNo (barcode) and Total MRP/Net Sale Price/Actual Disc are stored as floats in the source .xlsb; barcode must be cast via int() before str() to avoid scientific notation or trailing '.0'.
Sample sanity check (must hold in tests): MRP 2999, Net 1799 -> discount_value 1200, computed discount_pct 40% (matches supplied WAD).

Confirmed in real data: a single Pepe upload can span multiple months (the real DSR extract spans Jan-Apr 2026 in one file) -- slice detection must group by (store, month) actually present in the parsed data, never assume one file = one month.
